name: MSSQL SPProcoption Set
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
SIGMA Title: MSSQL SPProcoption Set
SIGMA Description: Detects when the a stored procedure is set or cleared for automatic execution in MSSQL. A stored procedure that is set to automatic execution runs every time an instance of SQL Server is started

False positives:
- Legitimate use of the feature by administrators (rare)

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=12 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%mssql%' AND "Event ID"=33205 AND (LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%object_name:sp_procoption%' OR LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%statement:exec%') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY MSSQL SPProcoption Set on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event matches devicetype=12 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%mssql%' AND "Event ID"=33205 AND (LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%object_name:sp_procoption%' OR LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%statement:exec%') AQL filter query
